LARGE PINE TREE BLOWN DOWN

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT A MICROBURST WITH WINDS UP TO 70 MPH IMPACTED A NARROW CORRIDOR THROUGH THE CENTRAL PORTION OF THE CITY OF SKIDMORE. THE AXIS OF WIND DAMAGE WAS CONCENTRATED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF HIGHWAY 181 AND WEST MAIN STREET. THE PERMITTER FENSE OF THE HIGH SCHOOL BASE BALL FIELD WAS TORN DOWN. A PORTION OF A ROOF WAS LIFTED OFF A HOME NEAR N 7TH STREET AND E MATHIS ST...AND A LARGE TREE LIMB WAS BLOWN DOWN ON 2ND STREET.
WATER OVER THE ROADWAY AT FM 1360 AND FM 3037 IN THE BONNIEVIEW AREA.

COCORAHS OBSERVER REPORTED THAT TREE BRANCHES WERE BLOWN OFF TREES AT CALLAGHAN RANCH. THE DIAMETER OF THE TREE BRANCHES WAS AROUND EIGHT INCHES.
COCORAHS OBSERVER REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL AT CALLAGHAN RANCH WHICH LASTED FOR AROUND 10 MINUTES.
COCORAHS OBSERVER RELAYED A REPORT OF SEVERE ROOF DAMAGE TO A 5000 SQUARE FOOT WAREHOUSE...DEER BLINDS BLOWN OVER...AND SHINGLES BLOWN OFF OF A HOME AT CALLAGHAN RANCH.
COCORAHS OBSERVER RELAYED A REPORT OF GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL AT CALLAGHAN RANCH.

60 MPH STRAIGHT LINE WIND SWEPT THROUGH FREER. NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM REPORTED A COUPLE OF STRUCTURES LOST THEIR ROOF. LARGE TREES DOWN. ONE HOUSE UNDER CONSTRUCTION WITH JUST THE WOOD FRAME COLLAPSED.

FREER FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTS ROOF DAMAGE TO HOME AND BUSINESS IN FREER. SEVERAL TIN SHEDS DAMAGED AS WELL AS POWERLINES DOWN. POWER OUT IN THE CITY. HAIL ALSO REPORTED JUST NORTH OF FREER...SIZE UNKNOWN.
QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL REPORTED JUST NORTH OF FREER. HAIL DEPTH ON THE GROUND WAS 2-3 INCHES.

NWS SURVEY COMBINED WITH FIRE CHIEF REPORTS INDICATES THAT GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL FELL IN NORTHEAST FREER CAUSING DENTS IN MOTOR VEHICLES...INCLUDING A NEW/USED VEHICLE LOT. A FEW BASEBALL SIZED HAIL STONES WERE OBSERVED AND BROKE A FEW WINDOWS IN NORTH FACING BUILDINGS.

BASEBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN FREER FROM FIRE CHIEF. WINDSHIELDS BROKEN OUT OF FIRE TRUCK. UPDATE IS TO INCREASE SIZE FROM BASEBALL TO SOFTBALL PER FIRE CHIEF. SEVERAL CARS DAMAGED FROM HAIL IN FREER.
BASEBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED IN FREER FROM FIRE CHIEF. WINDSHIELDS BROKEN OUT OF FIRE TRUCK.
QUARTER TO GOLFBALL SIZED HAIL REPORT IN AND JUST NORTH OF ALICE.
BASEBALL SIZE HAIL REPORTED BY FIRE CHIEF JUST NORTH OF ALICE.
HAIL BUSTED WINDOWS OF VEHICLES AND DESTROYED SOLAR PANELS.

*** 1 INJ *** NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT BASEBALL TO SOFTBALL SIZED FELL ACROSS BENAVIDES CAUSING DAMAGE TO NEARLY EVERY HOME AND BUSINESS IN THE TOWN. DAMAGE INCLUDED BROKEN WINDOWS...DOORS...ROOFS AND SIDING AND WAS CONFINED PRIMARILY TO THE NORTH AND EAST SIDE OF STRUCTURES. VEHICLES WERE ALSO SEVERELY DENTED WITH LARGE HOLES IN WINDSHIELDS OR COMPLETELY BROKEN OUT. ONE PERSON SUFFERED A HEAD INJURY.
BASEBALL SIZE HAIL AND STRONG WINDS IN BENAVIDES WITH MANY WINDOWS BROKEN IN TOWN.

SEVERAL EYEWITNESSES OBSERVED A TORNADO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF STATE HIGHWAY 188 AND FM 631. THE TORNADO REMAINED OVER AN OPEN FIELD FOR UP TO TEN MINUTES...PRODUCING NO SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE OR INJURIES.
